today.criticalsPending: Today will count when all critical habits are done ({remaining} of {total} left)
today.anyPending: Today will count when you complete any habit

diamondExtra.islamic: One year of consistency. May this be the beginning, not the peak.
diamondExtra.universal: One year of consistency. The first year is the hardest. Carry on.

spark.islamic: Every journey to Allah begins with a single step.
spark.universal: Every great fire began as a single spark.

flame.islamic: Seven days. The Prophet ﷺ said the most beloved deeds to Allah are the consistent ones — and you just proved it.
flame.universal: Seven days. You showed up when most people quit by day three. This is who you really are.

burn.islamic: Twenty-one days. Allah opens the door for those who knock with consistency. The habit is now wired into you.
burn.universal: Twenty-one days. Your brain has wired the habit. It's no longer something you do — it's becoming who you are.

blaze.islamic: Two months. You no longer choose this each day — you ARE someone who does this. Alhamdulillah.
blaze.universal: Two months. You stopped trying and started being. The identity is real now.

inferno.islamic: Four months. Allah loves the consistent, even if small. You are now of those He loves.
inferno.universal: Four months. While others started and quit a dozen times, you became unstoppable. This is rare.

sacred.islamic: By Allah's mercy, you are no longer building a habit — you are living an identity. The year-mark is near.
sacred.universal: Most people never reach this. You are no longer building a habit — you ARE this habit. The year-mark is near.

diamond.islamic: One full year. The fire of effort has cooled into something eternal. By Allah's mercy, you have become this habit — and it has become you.
diamond.universal: One full year. The fire is gone. What remains is permanent. You did not just build a habit — you forged who you are.
